name: Prompt to Image
description: AI models are cheaper at processing images than they are text
a11yLabel: Prompt to Image — paste or drop text on the canvas and it is laid out to fit inside one small image

inputs.label: Text
inputs.placeholder: Paste or drop text on the canvas…
inputs.help: Paste or drop anywhere on the canvas to fill this.
inputs.default:
You are a release-notes editor.

Input: a list of merged pull request titles with their labels.
Output: a single markdown document, grouped under these headings, in this order:

  ## Added
  ## Changed
  ## Fixed
  ## Removed

Rules:
- One bullet per user-visible change. Skip anything labelled `chore`, `ci`, or `deps` unless it changes behaviour someone outside the team would notice.
- Write each bullet in the present tense, describing the effect on the user — not the mechanics of the patch. "Exports keep their original filename" beats "refactor filename handling in export.ts".
- Merge duplicates. If three PRs fix one bug, that is one bullet.
- Never invent a change that is not in the input. If a title is too vague to summarise, quote it verbatim and add `(needs description)`.
- Omit a heading entirely when it has no bullets. Do not write "None".

End with a `## Upgrade notes` section only when something is breaking. Describe what breaks and the smallest edit that fixes it.

inputs.label: Columns
inputs.help: Auto picks whatever packs the text at the largest size.

inputs.label: Keep line breaks
inputs.help: On preserves the original line breaks — right for code, lists and structured prompts. Off reflows each paragraph to fill the width, which fits more in.
